script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. Wow, already June 24th, 2026. Time flies, huh?
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。わあ、もう2026年6月24日ですね。あっという間ですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It really does, Alex. Good morning. How are you doing today?
script.ja: 本当にね、アレックス。おはよう。調子はどう？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I'm okay. Just waiting for my coffee to kick in. You know how it is. It's The Daily Tanu-chan Show, and I'm still half asleep.
script.ja: まあまあです。コーヒーが効き始めるのを待ってるだけです。分かりますよね。ザ・デイリー・たぬちゃん・ショーなのに、まだ半分寝てるんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I can imagine. I've got my tea here. Looks like it's going to be an overcast day in Tokyo.
script.ja: 想像できるわ。私は紅茶ね。東京は曇りの一日になりそうだわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, really? I didn't even check. Is it going to rain? I'm hoping it stays dry for my commute later.
script.ja: ああ、そうなんですか？見てなかったです。雨降るんですかね？今日の帰り道は降らないといいんですけど。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Rain probability is only 14%. High of 25.2°C, low of 18.0°C. So, pretty mild, actually.
script.ja: 降水確率は14%だけよ。最高が25.2度、最低が18.0度。だから、実際はかなり穏やかね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's good. Mild is good. I'm always worried about the baby getting too hot or too cold when we're out.
script.ja: それはいいですね。穏やかなのはいい。出かけるとき、いつも赤ちゃんが暑くなりすぎたり寒くなりすぎたりしないか心配なんですよね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They adapt quickly, but it's natural to worry. Hey, speaking of big news, I was just reading about something that happened 'On This Day' back in 2022.
script.ja: すぐに順応するわよ、でも心配になるのは自然なことね。ねえ、大きなニュースといえば、2022年の「今日」に起こったことを読んでたのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh? What was that?
script.ja: へえ？何だったんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in a case called Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization. Basically, it overturned Roe v. Wade and Planned Parenthood v. Casey.
script.ja: 米国最高裁判所が、ドブス対ジャクソン女性健康機構という訴訟で判決を下したの。要するに、ロー対ウェイド判決とプランニング・ペアレントフッド対ケイシー判決を覆したのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, that. I remember hearing about that. So, it meant each state could decide its own abortion laws, right?
script.ja: ああ、あれですか。それ、聞いた覚えがあります。つまり、各州が独自の人工妊娠中絶法を決められるようになった、ってことですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's right. The authority went back to the individual states. It led to a lot of different laws starting to proliferate across the country.
script.ja: その通りよ。権限が各州に戻されたの。それが原因で、国中にたくさんの異なる法律が『増殖』し始めたわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Proliferate? What does that mean?
script.ja: プロリフェレイト？どういう意味ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It means to increase rapidly in number. Like, a lot of different laws started popping up everywhere.
script.ja: 急速に数が増えるってこと。まるで、たくさんの異なる法律が至る所でどんどん現れ始めた、って感じね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. So things just got more complicated depending on where you lived. That's not going to change at my office, thankfully. But I can imagine it was a big deal there.
script.ja: ああ、なるほど。住む場所によって物事がもっと複雑になった、と。それはうちの会社では変わらないでしょうけど、向こうでは大変なことだったでしょうね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It was. It definitely made a lot of headlines for a long time.
script.ja: そうね。しばらくの間、たくさんの見出しを飾ったのは確かよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah. Speaking of things that are complicated… my daughter is starting to get really expressive now. She’s just turned one, and she has definite opinions. Sometimes she gets really frustrated when she can’t get what she wants.
script.ja: ええ。複雑なことといえば…うちの娘が最近、本当に表情豊かになってきて。ちょうど1歳になったばかりなんですけど、はっきりとした意見があるみたいで。欲しいものが手に入らないと、すごくイライラする時があるんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Oh, the 'terrible twos' are just around the corner, then. Or maybe the 'frustrating ones.'
script.ja: あら、『魔の2歳児』がもうすぐそこね。それか、『イライラする1歳児』かしら。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh no. I read something recently, a parenting tip, about toddlers and challenging behavior. It said between two and three, they really start to assert themselves because they realize they’re separate individuals.
script.ja: ええ、まさか。最近、子育てのヒントを読んだんです、幼児の困った行動について。2歳から3歳の間に、自分は親とは別の個人だと気づいて、本当に自己主張し始めるって書いてありました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's very true. They're testing boundaries. It's a normal part of development.
script.ja: それは本当にそうよ。境界線を試しているの。成長の正常な一部よ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right. But the article was also about how to respond. It talked about setting age-appropriate limits to help 'alleviate' their frustration and teach them.
script.ja: ですよね。でも、記事は対処法についても書かれていました。彼らの欲求不満を『和らげる』手助けをして、教えるために、年齢に応じた制限を設けることについてでした。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Alleviate means to make something less severe or easier to bear. So, making their frustration not quite so bad.
script.ja: 『和らげる』というのは、何かを深刻でなくしたり、より耐えやすくしたりすることよ。だから、彼らの欲求不満をそんなにひどくなくする、ってことね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Got it. So, not just letting them do whatever they want, but also not getting angry back. It’s tricky. I’m already trying to practice.
script.ja: 分かりました。つまり、好きなようにさせるだけじゃなくて、こっちも怒り返さない、と。難しいですね。もう練習し始めてます。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. My kids are teenagers now, and they still have strong opinions. They just express them more... verbosely.
script.ja: そうね。うちの子たちはもうティーンエイジャーだけど、いまだに強い意見を持ってるわ。ただ、もっと…言葉多く表現するだけね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Verbosely? Is that another one of those words?
script.ja: バーボーズリー？それもまたそういう言葉ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, it means using too many words. Not very succinct, in other words.
script.ja: ええ、言葉数が多すぎるって意味よ。言い換えれば、あまり簡潔じゃない、ってことね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, okay. So, not succinct. I'll remember that for when she's older and telling me all about her day. Thanks, Sarah. This was helpful.
script.ja: ああ、分かりました。簡潔じゃない、と。娘がもっと大きくなって、一日の出来事を全部話してくれるようになった時のために覚えておきます。ありがとう、サラさん。役に立ちました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You're welcome, Alex. Good luck with the practice. We'll talk again soon.
script.ja: どういたしまして、アレックス。練習頑張ってね。またすぐ話しましょう。

wikipedia.text: In Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization, the U.S. Supreme Court rules that the U.S. Constitution does not assign the authority to regulate abortions to the federal government, thereby returning such authority to the individual states. This overturns the prior decisions in Roe v. Wade (1973) and Planned Parenthood v. Casey (1992).

parenting.title: Toddlers and Challenging Behavior: Why They Do It and How to Respond
parenting.summary: This article explores the meaning behind challenging behaviors and how parents and caregivers can set age-appropriate limits for their toddlers. The year between age 2 and age 3 is an exciting one. Toddlers are realizing that they are separate individuals from their parents and caregivers. This means that they are driven to assert themselves, to communicate their likes and dislikes, […]</
